rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BAKKIE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 29, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for Summary Adjudication granted as to issues one, two, and three that are unopposed. Motion denied as to issues four and five. CCP section 437c(f)(1) does not permit summary adjudication of a single item of compensatory damage which does not dispose of an entire cause of action. DeCastro West Chodorow & Burns, Inc. vs. Superior Court (1996) 47 Cal.App. 4th 410, 421-422. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JON SIEGAL VS. SOLIDUS NETWORKS INC A CORPORATION DOING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 29, 2009, line 5, PLAINTIFF JON SIEGAL Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Motion granted. Amendments to the pleadings are liberally permitted in the furtherance of justice. CCP section 473(a), 576. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HIN LAND INVESTMENT LIMITED, A HONG KONG VS. GORDON UHLMANN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motionf Or Leave To File First Amended X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 29, 2009, line 7, CROSS COMPLAINANT GORDON UHLMANN Motion for Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint. Motion granted. California has a policy of liberally granting amendments to pleadings. No inexcuseable delay or prejudice shown. Thirty days leave granted for Defendant to file responsive pleadings. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM F. RUIZ VS. KEVIN K. CURRIER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 29, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT USAA CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. The insured and the insurer cannot be joined as defendants in the same lawsuit. See Geraci vs. United Services Automobile Association (1987) 188 Cal.App. 3d 1245. =(302/CWW)
